vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely invalidated by the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct specimens. P3 (Pothos) is present but in a state of severe decline/dormancy.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No plant material visible in the yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No identifiable mint foliage present.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present: Pothos anchor (white rabbit) is visible, but the plant itself is severely compromised.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No identifiable silver guest foliage present.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.P2: Soil surface shows no active growth; potential desiccation.
plant_audit.P3: The Pothos specimen is severely etiolated or dead; only the white rabbit anchor remains as a structural marker.
plant_audit.P4: No evidence of the silver guest specimen.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark, potentially over-saturated or lacking drainage; no signs of fungal fruiting bodies, but high moisture retention is noted.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of white granular material (likely perlite or eggshell fragments) on the surface of P3.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Presence of a white cylindrical object (likely a sensor or bottle) introduced in the later images.

temporal_deltas.EARLIEST_to_T-4: Initial state shows a single green sprout in P3; P1 is empty.
temporal_deltas.T-4_to_T-3: Introduction of a metallic object (spoon) near P1.
temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-2: Introduction of a white cylindrical object (sensor/bottle) near P1.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Significant drop in ambient light levels; visual data is obscured by darkness.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Return of light reveals no recovery; the sprout in P3 appears to have collapsed or withered.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of critical failure. The lack of chlorophyll-rich foliage and the presence of bare soil across all pots suggests a total loss of the registered specimens. The Pothos (P3) has likely succumbed to environmental stress or lack of light.
anomalies: Introduction of a white cylindrical sensor/bottle (T-3 to CURRENT).
Total absence of expected foliage in P1, P2, and P4.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical audit reveals a catastrophic loss of the registered plant collection. The environment appears to be suffering from severe neglect or unsuitable conditions, as evidenced by the lack of vegetative growth and the presence of only inert soil and structural anchors. The Pothos in P3, which showed a faint sign of life in the earliest image, has failed to thrive. The introduction of non-botanical items (spoon, sensor) suggests an attempt at monitoring or intervention that has not yet resulted in biological recovery.
